report_name: UK Police Launch Campaign as Ransomware Attacks on Businesses Surge

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware attacks reported in the UK involved encrypting business data to demand a ransom payment for decryption.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Phishing emails are cited as a primary initial access vector for ransomware attacks targeting UK businesses.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The campaign advises businesses to apply software updates, noting that many ransomware attacks exploit known vulnerabilities to gain initial access.

malware.name: Ransomware
malware.description: A general category of malware that encrypts files and demands payment for decryption. It is reported to be increasingly targeting UK businesses, especially SMEs in the manufacturing, scientific, and education sectors.

campaigns.name: National 'Don't Pay' Campaign
campaigns.description: A national awareness campaign initiated by the City of London Police and Report Fraud to advise UK businesses against paying ransoms and to promote proactive cyber defenses like backups and software patching.
campaigns.objective: Discourage ransom payments and improve cyber resilience among UK businesses.

identities.name: City of London Police
identities.description: UK law enforcement agency that co-launched a national campaign against ransomware based on new statistics.

identities.name: Report Fraud
identities.description: UK national fraud and cybercrime reporting service that co-launched the anti-ransomware campaign.

identities.name: National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC)
identities.description: UK government organization whose guidance to not pay ransoms is promoted by the national campaign.

identities.name: Small and Medium-Sized Enterprises (SMEs)
identities.description: Identified as the primary victims of ransomware in the UK, accounting for 175 of 323 reported incidents in the 2025-2026 fiscal year.

identities.name: Manufacturing Sector
identities.description: The most impacted industry sector in the UK, with 42 reported ransomware incidents.

identities.name: Scientific and Technical Sector
identities.description: A frequently targeted industry in the UK with 21 reported ransomware incidents, likely due to valuable intellectual property.

identities.name: Education Sector
identities.description: A frequently targeted industry in the UK with 19 reported ransomware incidents.

indicators.name: Ransomware Note File Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Ransomware Note File Creation
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b9a-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a common ransomware note file name 'RECOVER_YOUR_FILES.txt'.
references:
  - https://www.cityoflondon.police.uk/news/city-of-london/news/2026/june/dont-pay-the-ransom-warning-to-organisations-to-protect-themselves-from-ransomware-attacks-as-more-than-320-businesses-affected-last-year/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-30
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\RECOVER_YOUR_FILES.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a common ransomware note file name 'RECOVER_YOUR_FILES.txt', as mentioned in the pre-extracted observables.

indicators.name: Ransomware File Encryption Extension
indicators.pattern:
title: Ransomware File Encryption Extension
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-2b1a-0e9f8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects files being renamed with the '.locked' extension, a common indicator of ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://www.cityoflondon.police.uk/news/city-of-london/news/2026/june/dont-pay-the-ransom-warning-to-organisations-to-protect-themselves-from-ransomware-attacks-as-more-than-320-businesses-affected-last-year/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-30
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.locked'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with the '.locked' extension, a common indicator of ransomware activity, based on pre-extracted observables.

indicators.name: Potential Brute Force Activity via Failed Logons
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Brute Force Activity via Failed Logons
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects Windows Event ID 4625, indicating a failed logon attempt. A high volume of these events can be a precursor to a brute force attack, a potential vector for ransomware.
references:
  - https://www.cityoflondon.police.uk/news/city-of-london/news/2026/june/dont-pay-the-ransom-warning-to-organisations-to-protect-themselves-from-ransomware-attacks-as-more-than-320-businesses-affected-last-year/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-30
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate failed logon attempts due to user error. Correlation with other events or volume thresholds is recommended.
level: low
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4625
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects Windows Event ID 4625 (failed logon), which was listed as a low-confidence observable. A high volume of these events can indicate brute force attempts.
